response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack engineer with a strong track record, but his core expertise is firmly in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not C++. His C++ exposure is limited to a six-month role at National Instruments over 25 years ago and a bachelor thesis side project, which falls far short of the deep, production-grade C++ proficiency this role demands. There is no evidence of multi-threading, STL, message-broker architecture in C++, MQTT, industrial automation protocols (OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850), or familiarity with IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle standards — all of which are central to this position.
strengths: Decades of software engineering experience including event-driven and distributed systems design
Exposure to low-latency, high-throughput systems (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby micro-services handling millions of requests/second)
Broad architectural thinking across embedded, web, and platform domains with agile practices
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — a single 6-month role from 2001 and a thesis project; no modern C++ or STL depth evident
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, or industrial protocol experience anywhere in the resume
Primary skill set (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) does not align with the core technical requirements of this role
